name: Seafood Risotto from Southern Living
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel shrimp and devein if desired; set aside.
2. Cut sea scallops in half crosswise; set aside.
3. Rinse squid under cold running water. Cut into 1/2-inch slices; set aside.
4. Remove "beards" from mussels and scrub mussels and clams with a brush to remove grit; set aside.
5. Cook garlic in olive oil over medium heat, stirring constantly, until shrimp turn pink. Remove shrimp and scallops with a slotted spoon and set aside.
6. Cook squid in the skillet over medium heat, stirring constantly, until it turns opaque. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside.
7. Add mussels, clams, wine, and the next three ingredients to the skillet. Bring to a boil over high heat.
8. Cover and cook, shaking the pan frequently, for 3 to 5 minutes or until shells open. Discard unopened shells.
9. Add shrimp, scallops, and squid. Cook over medium heat until thoroughly heated.
10. Remove seafood with a slotted spoon and reserve liquid. Stir the liquid into the cooked risotto.
11. Place risotto on a lettuce-lined serving plate and top with seafood mixture. Sprinkle with Gremolata, salt, and pepper; serve immediately.
1 pound sea scallops or shrimp may be substituted for squid.
Source: Southern Living, November, 1995
Typed by J. Matthews, Nov. 26, 1995
